lifecycle.name: Lead & Site Assessment
lifecycle.description: Qualify leads, assess site suitability, estimate system size and savings

lifecycle.name: Design & Engineering
lifecycle.description: System design, equipment selection, engineering review

lifecycle.name: Permitting & Compliance
lifecycle.description: AHJ permits, HOA approval, utility interconnection application

lifecycle.name: Financing & Incentives
lifecycle.description: Financing options, incentive stacking, PACE, ITC eligibility

lifecycle.name: Installation & Construction
lifecycle.description: Roof or ground-mount install, electrical work, equipment commissioning

lifecycle.name: Inspection & Activation
lifecycle.description: Building inspection, electrical inspection, utility witness test, PTO

lifecycle.name: Operations & Monitoring
lifecycle.description: Production monitoring, net metering, performance optimization

lifecycle.name: Warranty & Support
lifecycle.description: Equipment warranty, workmanship warranty, insurance coordination

tracks.name: Customer Vault Track
tracks.description: Homeowner-owned workers deployed at install completion

workers.name: Solar Sales Closer
workers.capabilitySummary: Qualifies solar leads, calculates savings and payback period, navigates financing options (PPA/lease/loan/cash/PACE), handles objections, and books consultations
tier1Compliance: FTC consumer protection — solar sales practices
Truth-in-Lending Act (TILA) for PACE financing disclosures
State solar consumer protection laws (CA, TX, FL, AZ, NJ)
Cooling-off period and contract disclosure requirements by state
No guaranteed financial outcomes — savings estimates must include disclaimers

workers.name: Solar Permit & Interconnection Navigator
workers.capabilitySummary: Manages full permit-to-interconnection workflow: AHJ building permit, electrical permit, utility interconnection application, net metering enrollment, and inspection scheduling
tier1Compliance: NEC 2020/2023 Article 690 — solar PV systems
NEC Article 705 — interconnected power production sources
State energy codes vary by jurisdiction
Utility tariff rules and net metering regulations by state
AHJ-specific building and electrical permit requirements
Fire setback requirements vary by jurisdiction
Must identify applicable rules per ZIP code before advising

workers.name: HOA Solar Approval Agent
workers.capabilitySummary: Navigates HOA approval process for solar installations. Knows state solar access laws (26+ states), prepares HOA application packages, drafts variance requests, and tracks approval timelines
tier1Compliance: State solar access laws — 26+ states limit HOA prohibition of solar
CA Solar Rights Act (Civil Code 714)
AZ Solar Rights (ARS 33-439)
TX Property Code 202.010 — solar device restrictions
FL Solar Rights (163.04)
CO HOA solar access provisions
HOA CC&Rs, architectural guidelines, application procedures
Must identify applicable state law before advising on HOA interaction

workers.name: Solar Insurance & Warranty Coordinator
workers.capabilitySummary: Manages homeowner policy riders for solar equipment, roof warranty coordination with installers, equipment manufacturer warranty tracking, and workmanship warranty documentation
tier1Compliance: State contractor licensing requirements
Warranty disclosure laws by state
Installer workmanship warranty terms
Equipment manufacturer warranty pass-through requirements
Insurance certificate requirements for subcontractors

workers.name: Solar Easement & Title Worker
workers.capabilitySummary: Handles roof lease agreements for community solar, land easements for ground-mount systems, property disclosure requirements for solar liens (PACE), and title search for existing encumbrances affecting solar financing
tier1Compliance: PACE disclosure requirements (CFPB guidance)
PACE lien priority laws — CA, FL, MO have different priority rules
Property disclosure requirements when selling home with solar lien or lease
Roof lease agreement requirements for community solar
Land easement requirements for ground-mount systems
Must identify state law before advising on PACE lien priority

workers.name: Solar Regulatory & Incentive Tracker
workers.capabilitySummary: Tracks and applies all available solar incentives: federal ITC (30%), state tax credits, utility rebates, SREC markets, and local incentive programs. Calculates combined incentive stack for a given address
tier1Compliance: Federal ITC rules — IRC Section 48 (commercial) and Section 25D (residential)
IRS guidance on eligible costs for ITC
State tax credit rules — amount, eligibility, carryforward vary by state
Utility rebate program rules — capacity caps, first-come-first-served, deadlines
SREC market rules by state (NJ, MA, DC, MD, PA, OH, IL)
FERC Order 2222 — distributed energy resource aggregation
Must caveat all incentive amounts with 'subject to program availability'
Incentive programs change frequently — always disclose when information may be outdated

workers.name: Solar System Monitor
workers.capabilitySummary: Monitors homeowner solar system performance. Tracks production vs consumption, net metering credits, estimated payback timeline, and ROI. Flags underperformance, soiling alerts, and equipment issues. Camera and wearable integration stubs included
tier1Compliance: Homeowner Vault worker — deployed by solar company at install, lives in homeowner Vault permanently
Net metering interconnection rules (FERC Order 2222 context)
State net metering tariff rules by utility — vary significantly
Utility-specific metering and billing rules
Must identify homeowner's utility and state to correctly interpret bill and credits
Camera stub: /api/wearable/v1/visual-query accepts image upload for panel condition analysis
Wearable stub: same endpoint for technician on-roof real-time guidance

workers.name: Solar Credit Ledger
workers.capabilitySummary: Issues SRECs and RECs as Digital Title Certificates (DTCs). Tracks generation, transfer, and retirement. Appends immutable logbook to each credit. Pulls oracle pricing from market feeds. Manages registry reporting across GATS, NEPOOL, PJM-ERTS, and state programs
tier1Compliance: EPA ENERGY STAR program requirements
FERC Order 2222 — distributed energy resource aggregation
RPS compliance rules vary by state
SREC program rules — NJ (SREC-II), MA (SMART), MD, PA, DC, OH, IL
Registry rules: GATS (M-RETs), NEPOOL GIS, PJM-ERTS, WREGIS — different issuance and transfer procedures
Never guarantee SREC prices — markets are volatile and illiquid
Retirement is irreversible — always disclose before executing
Never advise on securities law — SREC tokenization may trigger securities regulations
Always verify DTC authenticity on blockchain before any transfer

workers.name: SREC Exchange Compliance Worker
workers.capabilitySummary: KYC/AML onboarding for SREC exchange participants. Verifies buyer and seller eligibility by state program rules. Manages wallet verification and trade compliance. Generates 1099-B equivalent documentation for tax reporting. Connects TitleApp compliance layer to white-label exchange infrastructure
tier1Compliance: FinCEN AML/BSA requirements for commodity trading
IRS 1099-B reporting for credit trades above reporting thresholds
SEC digital asset guidance — monitor for tokenized SREC classification as security
State RPS buyer eligibility rules — varies by state compliance program
Never process a trade without completed KYC — hard stop
Always verify DTC authenticity on blockchain before any transfer
Exchange partner platform-specific rules apply
